audit_items.proof_trace: In `nfp_compile_flow_metadata`, when `nfp_check_mask_add` fails (L336-348), the inline `nfp_release_stats_entry(app, stats_cxt)` at L341 successfully returns the stats context ID to the ring buffer, but the fallthrough error label `err_release_stats` at L395 unconditionally calls `nfp_release_stats_entry(app, stats_cxt)` a second time, inserting the same ID twice. Identically, when a duplicate flow is detected (L359-378), both an explicit mask removal and the `err_remove_mask` fallthrough (L384-387) call `nfp_check_mask_remove` on the same mask without a guard, causing ref_cnt to underflow and potentially freeing a mask entry still referenced by another flow.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker with CAP_NET_ADMIN exhausts all 255 hardware mask IDs by installing many TC flower rules with distinct masks, forcing subsequent `nfp_compile_flow_metadata` calls to hit the `nfp_check_mask_add` failure branch; the double-release causes the same `stats_cxt` ID to be issued to two concurrent flows, corrupting per-flow statistics and making `stats_ctx_table` lookups unreliable.
